revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後方墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後方墳]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] infobox | image = [[File:Utsunomiya Shrine.JPG|300px|Taken in [[October 2009]].]] | name = Utsunomiya Shrine | location = {{ill|Komaki, Aichi|ja|小牧市}}, Ogi 3-chome 226 | coordinates = {{coord|35|16|46.09|N|136|53|46.26|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名}} | rank = {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.jinjahoncho.or.jp/5990|title=Utsunomiya Shrine|publisher=Association of Shinto Shrines|date=2020-03-12|accessdate=2022-04-04}}</ref> | establishment = | main_hall = | annual_festival = | rituals = }}
[[File:Utsunomiya Shrine 08.JPG|thumb|right|250px|Main shrine building.]]
'''Utsunomiya Shrine''' (宇都宮神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Komaki, Aichi|ja|小牧市}}, [[Aichi Prefecture]]. The shrine is notable for being the site of one of the largest {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds|ja|前方後方墳}} in the Owari region, known as the '''Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun'''.
== History == * Late [[3rd century]] to early [[4th century]] - Construction of the {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳}}. * Around [[1929]] (Showa 4) - Discovery of a {{ill|Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}}. * [[September 9, 1987]] (Showa 62) - The kofun was designated a [[Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture]], and the mirror was designated a [[Cultural Property of Aichi Prefecture]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/kouko/kensitei/0733.html|title=Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror|publisher=Aichi Prefecture|accessdate=2013-05-22}}</ref>
== Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site | name = Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun | location = | coordinates = {{coord|35|16|46.4|N|136|53|46.8|E|region:JP|display=inline}} | shape = Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound | scale = Total length: 59m; height: approximately 6.5m; rear section: approximately 35m on each side | burial_facility = | artifacts = | associated_mounds = | construction_period = Late 3rd century to early 4th century | buried_individual = | imperial_tombs = | historic_site = Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture | tangible_cultural_property = Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror (designated by Aichi Prefecture) }}
Located within the grounds of Utsunomiya Shrine, this is one of the largest {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds|ja|前方後方墳}} in the Owari region. Part of the {{ill|Ogi Kofun Cluster|ja|小木古墳群}}, it was constructed in the late [[3rd century]] to early [[4th century]]. The presence of the kofun indicates the prominence of a powerful local clan during that era. In addition to this kofun, several others exist in the vicinity. The mound is 59m in total length, 6.5m in height, and approximately 35m on each side of the rear section. A vertical stone chamber is located within. The site was designated a Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture in [[1987]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/kinenbutu/siseki/kensitei/0923.html|title=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|publisher=Aichi Prefecture|accessdate=2013-05-22}}</ref>
<gallery> File:Utsunomiya Jinjya Kofun 03.JPG|Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == * Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror (三角縁獣文帯三神三獣鏡) - A [[bronze mirror]] excavated from the Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun. It was discovered around [[1929]] during the construction of the shrine hall at the front of the kofun and designated a Cultural Property of Aichi Prefecture in [[1987]].
== location == {{ill|Komaki, Aichi|ja|小牧市}}, Ogi 3-chome 226.
== Access == * 5-minute walk from the {{ill|Ogi bus stop|ja|小木バス停留所}} served by {{ill|Meitetsu Bus|ja|名鉄バス}}.
== Nearby landmarks == * {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Road 25|ja|愛知県道25号春日井一宮線}} * {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Road 158|ja|愛知県道158号小口名古屋線}} * {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Road 451|ja|愛知県道451号名古屋外環状線}} * Jōon-ji Temple * {{ill|Kōyashiki Kofun|ja|甲屋敷古墳}} * Seson-ji Temple * Shintsū-ji Temple * Ogi Elementary School (Komaki City)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related topics == * {{ill|Ogi Kofun Cluster|ja|小木古墳群}} - A general term for the group of kofun in this area. * {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine|ja|宇都宮神社}}
== External links == {{Commonscat|Utsunomiya Shrine}} {{Commonscat|Utsunomiya Jinjya Kofun|宇都宮神社古墳}} * [https://www.utunomiyajinjya-komaki.com/ Utsunomiya Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/2/9111.html Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun (Komaki City Hall)] * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/2/9115.html Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror (Komaki City Hall)] * [https://apec.aichi-c.ed.jp/kyouka/shakai/kyouzai/2018/syakai/owari/owa042.htm Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun (Aichi ACE Net)]
{{Shinto horizontal}} {{Redirect Category:kk]][[Category: association | redirect1 = Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun | 1-1 = Kofun in Aichi Prefecture | 1-2 = History of Komaki City | 1-3 = Historic Sites of Aichi Prefecture | 1-4 = Rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds }} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Former village shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Komaki City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Buildings and structures in Komaki City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Tangible cultural properties of Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pp]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Utsunomiya Shrine]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pp]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] infobox | image = [[File:Utsunomiya Shrine.JPG|300px|Taken in [[October 2009]].]] | name = Utsunomiya Shrine | location = {{ill|Komaki, Aichi|ja|小牧市}}, Ogi 3-chome 226 | coordinates = {{coord|35|16|46.09|N|136|53|46.26|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名}} | rank = {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.jinjahoncho.or.jp/5990|title=Utsunomiya Shrine|publisher=Association of Shinto Shrines|date=2020-03-12|accessdate=2022-04-04}}</ref> | establishment = | main_hall = | annual_festival = | rituals = }}
[[File:Utsunomiya Shrine 08.JPG|thumb|right|250px|Main shrine building.]]
'''Utsunomiya Shrine''' (宇都宮神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Komaki, Aichi|ja|小牧市}}, [[Aichi Prefecture]]. The shrine is notable for being the site of one of the largest {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds|ja|前方後方墳}} in the Owari region, known as the '''Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun'''.
== History == * Late [[3rd century]] to early [[4th century]] - Construction of the {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳}}. * Around [[1929]] (Showa 4) - Discovery of a {{ill|Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}}. * [[September 9, 1987]] (Showa 62) - The kofun was designated a [[Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture]], and the mirror was designated a [[Cultural Property of Aichi Prefecture]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/kouko/kensitei/0733.html|title=Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror|publisher=Aichi Prefecture|accessdate=2013-05-22}}</ref>
== Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site | name = Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun | location = | coordinates = {{coord|35|16|46.4|N|136|53|46.8|E|region:JP|display=inline}} | shape = Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound | scale = Total length: 59m; height: approximately 6.5m; rear section: approximately 35m on each side | burial_facility = | artifacts = | associated_mounds = | construction_period = Late 3rd century to early 4th century | buried_individual = | imperial_tombs = | historic_site = Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture | tangible_cultural_property = Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror (designated by Aichi Prefecture) }}
Located within the grounds of Utsunomiya Shrine, this is one of the largest {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds|ja|前方後方墳}} in the Owari region. Part of the {{ill|Ogi Kofun Cluster|ja|小木古墳群}}, it was constructed in the late [[3rd century]] to early [[4th century]]. The presence of the kofun indicates the prominence of a powerful local clan during that era. In addition to this kofun, several others exist in the vicinity. The mound is 59m in total length, 6.5m in height, and approximately 35m on each side of the rear section. A vertical stone chamber is located within. The site was designated a Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture in [[1987]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/kinenbutu/siseki/kensitei/0923.html|title=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|publisher=Aichi Prefecture|accessdate=2013-05-22}}</ref>
<gallery> File:Utsunomiya Jinjya Kofun 03.JPG|Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == * Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror (三角縁獣文帯三神三獣鏡) - A [[bronze mirror]] excavated from the Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun. It was discovered around [[1929]] during the construction of the shrine hall at the front of the kofun and designated a Cultural Property of Aichi Prefecture in [[1987]].
== location == {{ill|Komaki, Aichi|ja|小牧市}}, Ogi 3-chome 226.
== Access == * 5-minute walk from the {{ill|Ogi bus stop|ja|小木バス停留所}} served by {{ill|Meitetsu Bus|ja|名鉄バス}}.
== Nearby landmarks == * {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Road 25|ja|愛知県道25号春日井一宮線}} * {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Road 158|ja|愛知県道158号小口名古屋線}} * {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Road 451|ja|愛知県道451号名古屋外環状線}} * Jōon-ji Temple * {{ill|Kōyashiki Kofun|ja|甲屋敷古墳}} * Seson-ji Temple * Shintsū-ji Temple * Ogi Elementary School (Komaki City)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related topics == * {{ill|Ogi Kofun Cluster|ja|小木古墳群}} - A general term for the group of kofun in this area. * {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine|ja|宇都宮神社}}

The site was designated a Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture in [[1987]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/kinenbutu/siseki/kensitei/0923.html|title=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|publisher=Aichi Prefecture|accessdate=2013-05-22}}</ref>

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] infobox | image = [[File:Utsunomiya Shrine.JPG|300px|Taken in [[October 2009]].]] | name = Utsunomiya Shrine | location = {{ill|Komaki, Aichi|ja|小牧市}}, Ogi 3-chome 226 | coordinates = {{coord|35|16|46.09|N|136|53|46.26|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名}} | rank = {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.jinjahoncho.or.jp/5990|title=Utsunomiya Shrine|publisher=Association of Shinto Shrines|date=2020-03-12|accessdate=2022-04-04}}</ref> | establishment = | main_hall = | annual_festival = | rituals = }}
[[File:Utsunomiya Shrine 08.JPG|thumb|right|250px|Main shrine building.]]
'''Utsunomiya Shrine''' (宇都宮神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Komaki, Aichi|ja|小牧市}}, [[Aichi Prefecture]]. The shrine is notable for being the site of one of the largest {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} in the Owari region, known as the '''Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun'''.
== History == * Late [[3rd century]] to early [[4th century]] - Construction of the {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}. * Around [[1929]] (Showa 4) - Discovery of a {{ill|Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}}. * [[September 9, 1987]] (Showa 62) - The kofun was designated a [[Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture]], and the mirror was designated a [[Cultural Property of Aichi Prefecture]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/kouko/kensitei/0733.html|title=Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror|publisher=Aichi Prefecture|accessdate=2013-05-22}}</ref>
== Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site | name = Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun | location = | coordinates = {{coord|35|16|46.4|N|136|53|46.8|E|region:JP|display=inline}} | type = Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound | scale = Total length: 59m; height: approximately 6.5m; rear section: approximately 35m on each side | burial_facility = | artifacts = | associated_mounds = | construction_period = Late 3rd century to early 4th century | buried_individual = | imperial_tombs = | historic_site = Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture | tangible_cultural_property = Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror (designated by Aichi Prefecture) }}
Located within the grounds of Utsunomiya Shrine, this is one of the largest {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} in the Owari region. Part of the {{ill|Ogi Kofun Cluster|ja|小木古墳群}}, it was constructed in the late [[3rd century]] to early [[4th century]]. The presence of the kofun indicates the prominence of a powerful local clan during that era. In addition to this kofun, several others exist in the vicinity. The mound is 59m in total length, 6.5m in height, and approximately 35m on each side of the rear section. A vertical stone chamber is located within. The site was designated a Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture in [[1987]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/kinenbutu/siseki/kensitei/0923.html|title=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|publisher=Aichi Prefecture|accessdate=2013-05-22}}</ref>
<gallery> File:Utsunomiya Jinjya Kofun 03.JPG|Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == * Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror (三角縁獣文帯三神三獣鏡) - A [[bronze mirror]] excavated from the Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun. It was discovered around [[1929]] during the construction of the shrine hall at the front of the kofun and designated a Cultural Property of Aichi Prefecture in [[1987]].
== location == {{ill|Komaki, Aichi|ja|小牧市}}, Ogi 3-chome 226.
== Access == * 5-minute walk from the {{ill|Ogi bus stop|ja|小木バス停留所}} served by {{ill|Meitetsu Bus|ja|名鉄バス}}.
== Nearby landmarks == * {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Road 25|ja|愛知県道25号春日井一宮線}} * {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Road 158|ja|愛知県道158号小口名古屋線}} * {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Road 451|ja|愛知県道451号名古屋外環状線}} * Jōon-ji Temple * {{ill|Kōyashiki Kofun|ja|甲屋敷古墳}} * Seson-ji Temple * Shintsū-ji Temple * Ogi Elementary School (Komaki City)

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] infobox | image = [[File:Utsunomiya Shrine.JPG|300px|Taken in [[October 2009]].]] | name = Utsunomiya Shrine | location = {{ill|Komaki, Aichi|ja|小牧市}}, Ogi 3-chome 226 | coordinates = {{coord|35|16|46.09|N|136|53|46.26|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名}} | rank = {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.jinjahoncho.or.jp/5990|title=Utsunomiya Shrine|publisher=Association of Shinto Shrines|date=2020-03-12|accessdate=2022-04-04}}</ref> | establishment = | main_hall = | annual_festival = | rituals = }}
[[File:Utsunomiya Shrine 08.JPG|thumb|right|250px|Main shrine building.]]
'''Utsunomiya Shrine''' (宇都宮神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Komaki, Aichi|ja|小牧市}}, [[Aichi Prefecture]]. The shrine is notable for being the site of one of the largest {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} in the Owari region, known as the '''Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun'''.
== History == * Late [[3rd century]] to early [[4th century]] - Construction of the {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}. * Around [[1929]] (Showa 4) - Discovery of a {{ill|Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}}. * [[September 9, 1987]] (Showa 62) - The kofun was designated a [[Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture]], and the mirror was designated a [[Cultural Property of Aichi Prefecture]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/kouko/kensitei/0733.html|title=Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror|publisher=Aichi Prefecture|accessdate=2013-05-22}}</ref>
== Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site | name = Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun | location = | coordinates = {{coord|35|16|46.4|N|136|53|46.8|E|region:JP|display=inline}} | type = Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound | scale = Total length: 59m; height: approximately 6.5m; rear section: approximately 35m on each side | burial_facility = | artifacts = | associated_mounds = | construction_period = Late 3rd century to early 4th century | buried_individual = | imperial_tombs = | historic_site = Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture | tangible_cultural_property = Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror (designated by Aichi Prefecture) }}
Located within the grounds of Utsunomiya Shrine, this is one of the largest {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} in the Owari region. Part of the {{ill|Ogi Kofun Cluster|ja|小木古墳群}}, it was constructed in the late [[3rd century]] to early [[4th century]]. The presence of the kofun indicates the prominence of a powerful local clan during that era. In addition to this kofun, several others exist in the vicinity. The mound is 59m in total length, 6.5m in height, and approximately 35m on each side of the rear section. A vertical stone chamber is located within. The site was designated a Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture in [[1987]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/kinenbutu/siseki/kensitei/0923.html|title=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|publisher=Aichi Prefecture|accessdate=2013-05-22}}</ref>
<gallery> File:Utsunomiya Jinjya Kofun 03.JPG|Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == * Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror (三角縁獣文帯三神三獣鏡) - A [[bronze mirror]] excavated from the Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun. It was discovered around [[1929]] during the construction of the shrine hall at the front of the kofun and designated a Cultural Property of Aichi Prefecture in [[1987]].
== location == {{ill|Komaki, Aichi|ja|小牧市}}, Ogi 3-chome 226.
== Access == * 5-minute walk from the {{ill|Ogi bus stop|ja|小木バス停留所}} served by {{ill|Meitetsu Bus|ja|名鉄バス}}.
== Nearby landmarks == * {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Road 25|ja|愛知県道25号春日井一宮線}} * {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Road 158|ja|愛知県道158号小口名古屋線}} * {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Road 451|ja|愛知県道451号名古屋外環状線}} * Jōon-ji Temple * {{ill|Kōyashiki Kofun|ja|甲屋敷古墳}} * Seson-ji Temple * Shintsū-ji Temple * Ogi Elementary School (Komaki City)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related topics == * {{ill|Ogi Kofun Cluster|ja|小木古墳群}} - A general term for the group of kofun in this area. * {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine|ja|宇都宮神社}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] infobox | image = [[File:Utsunomiya Shrine.JPG|300px|Taken in [[October 2009]].]] | name = Utsunomiya Shrine | location = {{ill|Komaki, Aichi|ja|小牧市}}, Ogi 3-chome 226 | coordinates = {{coord|35|16|46.09|N|136|53|46.26|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名}} | rank = {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.jinjahoncho.or.jp/5990|title=Utsunomiya Shrine|publisher=Association of Shinto Shrines|date=2020-03-12|accessdate=2022-04-04}}</ref> | establishment = | main_hall = | annual_festival = | rituals = }}
[[File:Utsunomiya Shrine 08.JPG|thumb|right|250px|Main shrine building.]]
'''Utsunomiya Shrine''' (宇都宮神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Komaki, Aichi|ja|小牧市}}, [[Aichi Prefecture]]. The shrine is notable for being the site of one of the largest {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} in the Owari region, known as the '''Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun'''.
== History == * Late [[3rd century]] to early [[4th century]] - Construction of the {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}. * Around [[1929]] (Showa 4) - Discovery of a {{ill|Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}}. * [[September 9, 1987]] (Showa 62) - The kofun was designated a [[Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture]], and the mirror was designated a [[Cultural Property of Aichi Prefecture]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/kouko/kensitei/0733.html|title=Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror|publisher=Aichi Prefecture|accessdate=2013-05-22}}</ref>
== Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site | name = Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun | location = | coordinates = {{coord|35|16|46.4|N|136|53|46.8|E|region:JP|display=inline}} | type = Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound | scale = Total length: 59m; height: approximately 6.5m; rear section: approximately 35m on each side | burial_facility = | artifacts = | associated_mounds = | construction_period = Late 3rd century to early 4th century | buried_individual = | imperial_tombs = | historic_site = Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture | tangible_cultural_property = Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror (designated by Aichi Prefecture) }}
Located within the grounds of Utsunomiya Shrine, this is one of the largest {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} in the Owari region. Part of the {{ill|Ogi Kofun Cluster|ja|小木古墳群}}, it was constructed in the late [[3rd century]] to early [[4th century]]. The presence of the kofun indicates the prominence of a powerful local clan during that era. In addition to this kofun, several others exist in the vicinity. The mound is 59m in total length, 6.5m in height, and approximately 35m on each side of the rear section. A vertical stone chamber is located within. The site was designated a Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture in [[1987]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/kinenbutu/siseki/kensitei/0923.html|title=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|publisher=Aichi Prefecture|accessdate=2013-05-22}}</ref>
<gallery> File:Utsunomiya Jinjya Kofun 03.JPG|Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == * Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror (三角縁獣文帯三神三獣鏡) - A [[bronze mirror]] excavated from the Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun. It was discovered around [[1929]] during the construction of the shrine hall at the front of the kofun and designated a Cultural Property of Aichi Prefecture in [[1987]].
== location == {{ill|Komaki, Aichi|ja|小牧市}}, Ogi 3-chome 226.
== Access == * 5-minute walk from the {{ill|Ogi bus stop|ja|小木バス停留所}} served by {{ill|Meitetsu Bus|ja|名鉄バス}}.
== Nearby landmarks == * {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Road 25|ja|愛知県道25号春日井一宮線}} * {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Road 158|ja|愛知県道158号小口名古屋線}} * {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Road 451|ja|愛知県道451号名古屋外環状線}} * Jōon-ji Temple * {{ill|Kōyashiki Kofun|ja|甲屋敷古墳}} * Seson-ji Temple * Shintsū-ji Temple * Ogi Elementary School (Komaki City)

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] infobox | image = [[File:Utsunomiya Shrine.JPG|300px|Taken in [[October 2009]].]] | name = Utsunomiya Shrine | location = {{ill|Komaki, Aichi|ja|小牧市}}, Ogi 3-chome 226 | coordinates = {{coord|35|16|46.09|N|136|53|46.26|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名}} | rank = {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.jinjahoncho.or.jp/5990|title=Utsunomiya Shrine|publisher=Association of Shinto Shrines|date=2020-03-12|accessdate=2022-04-04}}</ref> | establishment = | main_hall = | annual_festival = | rituals = }}
[[File:Utsunomiya Shrine 08.JPG|thumb|right|250px|Main shrine building.]]
'''Utsunomiya Shrine''' (宇都宮神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Komaki, Aichi|ja|小牧市}}, [[Aichi Prefecture]]. The shrine is notable for being the site of one of the largest {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} in the Owari region, known as the '''Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun'''.
== History == * Late [[3rd century]] to early [[4th century]] - Construction of the {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}. * Around [[1929]] (Showa 4) - Discovery of a {{ill|Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}}. * [[September 9, 1987]] (Showa 62) - The kofun was designated a [[Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture]], and the mirror was designated a [[Cultural Property of Aichi Prefecture]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/kouko/kensitei/0733.html|title=Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror|publisher=Aichi Prefecture|accessdate=2013-05-22}}</ref>
== Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site | name = Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun | location = | coordinates = {{coord|35|16|46.4|N|136|53|46.8|E|region:JP|display=inline}} | type = Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound | scale = Total length: 59m; height: approximately 6.5m; rear section: approximately 35m on each side | burial_facility = | artifacts = | associated_mounds = | construction_period = Late 3rd century to early 4th century | buried_individual = | imperial_tombs = | historic_site = Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture | tangible_cultural_property = Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror (designated by Aichi Prefecture) }}
Located within the grounds of Utsunomiya Shrine, this is one of the largest {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} in the Owari region. Part of the {{ill|Ogi Kofun Cluster|ja|小木古墳群}}, it was constructed in the late [[3rd century]] to early [[4th century]]. The presence of the kofun indicates the prominence of a powerful local clan during that era. In addition to this kofun, several others exist in the vicinity. The mound is 59m in total length, 6.5m in height, and approximately 35m on each side of the rear section. A vertical stone chamber is located within. The site was designated a Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture in [[1987]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/kinenbutu/siseki/kensitei/0923.html|title=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|publisher=Aichi Prefecture|accessdate=2013-05-22}}</ref>
<gallery> File:Utsunomiya Jinjya Kofun 03.JPG|Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == * Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror (三角縁獣文帯三神三獣鏡) - A [[bronze mirror]] excavated from the Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun. It was discovered around [[1929]] during the construction of the shrine hall at the front of the kofun and designated a Cultural Property of Aichi Prefecture in [[1987]].
== location == {{ill|Komaki, Aichi|ja|小牧市}}, Ogi 3-chome 226.
== Access == * 5-minute walk from the {{ill|Ogi bus stop|ja|小木バス停留所}} served by {{ill|Meitetsu Bus|ja|名鉄バス}}.
== Nearby landmarks == * {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Road 25|ja|愛知県道25号春日井一宮線}} * {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Road 158|ja|愛知県道158号小口名古屋線}} * {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Road 451|ja|愛知県道451号名古屋外環状線}} * Jōon-ji Temple * {{ill|Kōyashiki Kofun|ja|甲屋敷古墳}} * Seson-ji Temple * Shintsū-ji Temple * Ogi Elementary School (Komaki City)
== References == {{Reflist}}

[[File:Utsunomiya Shrine 08.JPG|thumb|right|250px|Main shrine building.]]
'''Utsunomiya Shrine''' (宇都宮神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Komaki, Aichi|ja|小牧市}}, [[Aichi Prefecture]]. The shrine is notable for being the site of one of the largest {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} in the Owari region, known as the '''Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun'''.
== History == * Late [[3rd century]] to early [[4th century]] - Construction of the {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}. * Around [[1929]] (Showa 4) - Discovery of a {{ill|Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}}. * [[September 9, 1987]] (Showa 62) - The kofun was designated a [[Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture]], and the mirror was designated a [[Cultural Property of Aichi Prefecture]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/kouko/kensitei/0733.html|title=Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror|publisher=Aichi Prefecture|accessdate=2013-05-22}}</ref>
== Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site | name = Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun | location = | coordinates = {{coord|35|16|46.4|N|136|53|46.8|E|region:JP|display=inline}} | type = Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound | scale = Total length: 59m; height: approximately 6.5m; rear section: approximately 35m on each side | burial_facility = | artifacts = | associated_mounds = | construction_period = Late 3rd century to early 4th century | buried_individual = | imperial_tombs = | historic_site = Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture | tangible_cultural_property = Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror (designated by Aichi Prefecture) }}
Located within the grounds of Utsunomiya Shrine, this is one of the largest {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} in the Owari region. Part of the {{ill|Ogi Kofun Cluster|ja|小木古墳群}}, it was constructed in the late [[3rd century]] to early [[4th century]]. The presence of the kofun indicates the prominence of a powerful local clan during that era. In addition to this kofun, several others exist in the vicinity. The mound is 59m in total length, 6.5m in height, and approximately 35m on each side of the rear section. A vertical stone chamber is located within. The site was designated a Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture in [[1987]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/kinenbutu/siseki/kensitei/0923.html|title=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|publisher=Aichi Prefecture|accessdate=2013-05-22}}</ref>
<gallery> File:Utsunomiya Jinjya Kofun 03.JPG|Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == * Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror (三角縁獣文帯三神三獣鏡) - A [[bronze mirror]] excavated from the Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun. It was discovered around [[1929]] during the construction of the shrine hall at the front of the kofun and designated a Cultural Property of Aichi Prefecture in [[1987]].
== location == {{ill|Komaki, Aichi|ja|小牧市}}, Ogi 3-chome 226.
== Access == * 5-minute walk from the {{ill|Ogi bus stop|ja|小木バス停留所}} served by {{ill|Meitetsu Bus|ja|名鉄バス}}.

[[File:Utsunomiya Shrine 08.JPG|thumb|right|250px|Main shrine building.]]
'''Utsunomiya Shrine''' (宇都宮神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Komaki, Aichi|ja|小牧市}}, [[Aichi Prefecture]]. The shrine is notable for being the site of one of the largest {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} in the Owari region, known as the '''Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun'''.
== History == * Late [[3rd century]] to early [[4th century]] - Construction of the {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}. * Around [[1929]] (Showa 4) - Discovery of a {{ill|Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}}. * [[September 9, 1987]] (Showa 62) - The kofun was designated a [[Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture]], and the mirror was designated a [[Cultural Property of Aichi Prefecture]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/kouko/kensitei/0733.html|title=Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror|publisher=Aichi Prefecture|accessdate=2013-05-22}}</ref>
== Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site | name = Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun | location = | coordinates = {{coord|35|16|46.4|N|136|53|46.8|E|region:JP|display=inline}} | type = Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound | scale = Total length: 59m; height: approximately 6.5m; rear section: approximately 35m on each side | burial_facility = | artifacts = | associated_mounds = | construction_period = Late 3rd century to early 4th century | buried_individual = | imperial_tombs = | historic_site = Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture | tangible_cultural_property = Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror (designated by Aichi Prefecture) }}
Located within the grounds of Utsunomiya Shrine, this is one of the largest {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} in the Owari region. Part of the {{ill|Ogi Kofun Cluster|ja|小木古墳群}}, it was constructed in the late [[3rd century]] to early [[4th century]]. The presence of the kofun indicates the prominence of a powerful local clan during that era. In addition to this kofun, several others exist in the vicinity. The mound is 59m in total length, 6.5m in height, and approximately 35m on each side of the rear section. A vertical stone chamber is located within. The site was designated a Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture in [[1987]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/kinenbutu/siseki/kensitei/0923.html|title=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|publisher=Aichi Prefecture|accessdate=2013-05-22}}</ref>
<gallery> File:Utsunomiya Jinjya Kofun 03.JPG|Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == * Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror (三角縁獣文帯三神三獣鏡) - A [[bronze mirror]] excavated from the Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun. It was discovered around [[1929]] during the construction of the shrine hall at the front of the kofun and designated a Cultural Property of Aichi Prefecture in [[1987]].
== location == {{ill|Komaki, Aichi|ja|小牧市}}, Ogi 3-chome 226.
== Access == * 5-minute walk from the {{ill|Ogi bus stop|ja|小木バス停留所}} served by {{ill|Meitetsu Bus|ja|名鉄バス}}.
== Nearby landmarks == * {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Road 25|ja|愛知県道25号春日井一宮線}} * {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Road 158|ja|愛知県道158号小口名古屋線}} * {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Road 451|ja|愛知県道451号名古屋外環状線}} * Jōon-ji Temple * {{ill|Kōyashiki Kofun|ja|甲屋敷古墳}} * Seson-ji Temple * Shintsū-ji Temple * Ogi Elementary School (Komaki City)

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] infobox | image = [[File:Utsunomiya Shrine.JPG|300px|Taken in [[October 2009]].]] | name = Utsunomiya Shrine | location = {{ill|Komaki, Aichi|ja|小牧市}}, Ogi 3-chome 226 | coordinates = {{coord|35|16|46.09|N|136|53|46.26|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名}} | rank = {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.jinjahoncho.or.jp/5990|title=Utsunomiya Shrine|publisher=Association of Shinto Shrines|date=2020-03-12|accessdate=2022-04-04}}</ref> | establishment = | main_hall = | annual_festival = | rituals = }}
[[File:Utsunomiya Shrine 08.JPG|thumb|right|250px|Main shrine building.]]
'''Utsunomiya Shrine''' (宇都宮神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Komaki, Aichi|ja|小牧市}}, [[Aichi Prefecture]]. The shrine is notable for being the site of one of the largest {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} in the Owari region, known as the '''Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun'''.
== History == * Late [[3rd century]] to early [[4th century]] - Construction of the {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}. * Around [[1929]] (Showa 4) - Discovery of a {{ill|Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}}. * [[September 9, 1987]] (Showa 62) - The kofun was designated a [[Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture]], and the mirror was designated a [[Cultural Property of Aichi Prefecture]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/kouko/kensitei/0733.html|title=Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror|publisher=Aichi Prefecture|accessdate=2013-05-22}}</ref>
== Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site | name = Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun | location = | coordinates = {{coord|35|16|46.4|N|136|53|46.8|E|region:JP|display=inline}} | type = Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound | scale = Total length: 59m; height: approximately 6.5m; rear section: approximately 35m on each side | burial_facility = | artifacts = | associated_mounds = | construction_period = Late 3rd century to early 4th century | buried_individual = | imperial_tombs = | historic_site = Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture | tangible_cultural_property = Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror (designated by Aichi Prefecture) }}
Located within the grounds of Utsunomiya Shrine, this is one of the largest {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} in the Owari region. Part of the {{ill|Ogi Kofun Cluster|ja|小木古墳群}}, it was constructed in the late [[3rd century]] to early [[4th century]]. The presence of the kofun indicates the prominence of a powerful local clan during that era. In addition to this kofun, several others exist in the vicinity. The mound is 59m in total length, 6.5m in height, and approximately 35m on each side of the rear section. A vertical stone chamber is located within. The site was designated a Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture in [[1987]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/kinenbutu/siseki/kensitei/0923.html|title=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|publisher=Aichi Prefecture|accessdate=2013-05-22}}</ref>
<gallery> File:Utsunomiya Jinjya Kofun 03.JPG|Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == * Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror (三角縁獣文帯三神三獣鏡) - A [[bronze mirror]] excavated from the Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun. It was discovered around [[1929]] during the construction of the shrine hall at the front of the kofun and designated a Cultural Property of Aichi Prefecture in [[1987]].
== location == {{ill|Komaki, Aichi|ja|小牧市}}, Ogi 3-chome 226.
== Access == * 5-minute walk from the {{ill|Ogi bus stop|ja|小木バス停留所}} served by {{ill|Meitetsu Bus|ja|名鉄バス}}.
== Nearby landmarks == * {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Road 25|ja|愛知県道25号春日井一宮線}} * {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Road 158|ja|愛知県道158号小口名古屋線}} * {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Road 451|ja|愛知県道451号名古屋外環状線}} * Jōon-ji Temple * {{ill|Kōyashiki Kofun|ja|甲屋敷古墳}} * Seson-ji Temple * Shintsū-ji Temple * Ogi Elementary School (Komaki City)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related topics == * {{ill|Ogi Kofun Cluster|ja|小木古墳群}} - A general term for the group of kofun in this area. * {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine|ja|宇都宮神社}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] infobox | image = [[File:Utsunomiya Shrine.JPG|300px|Taken in [[October 2009]].]] | name = Utsunomiya Shrine | location = {{ill|Komaki, Aichi|ja|小牧市}}, Ogi 3-chome 226 | coordinates = {{coord|35|16|46.09|N|136|53|46.26|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名}} | rank = {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.jinjahoncho.or.jp/5990|title=Utsunomiya Shrine|publisher=Association of Shinto Shrines|date=2020-03-12|accessdate=2022-04-04}}</ref> | establishment = | main_hall = | annual_festival = | rituals = }}
[[File:Utsunomiya Shrine 08.JPG|thumb|right|250px|Main shrine building.]]
'''Utsunomiya Shrine''' (宇都宮神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Komaki, Aichi|ja|小牧市}}, [[Aichi Prefecture]]. The shrine is notable for being the site of one of the largest {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} in the Owari region, known as the '''Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun'''.
== History == * Late [[3rd century]] to early [[4th century]] - Construction of the {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}. * Around [[1929]] (Showa 4) - Discovery of a {{ill|Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}}. * [[September 9, 1987]] (Showa 62) - The kofun was designated a [[Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture]], and the mirror was designated a [[Cultural Property of Aichi Prefecture]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/kouko/kensitei/0733.html|title=Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror|publisher=Aichi Prefecture|accessdate=2013-05-22}}</ref>
== Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site | name = Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun | location = | coordinates = {{coord|35|16|46.4|N|136|53|46.8|E|region:JP|display=inline}} | type = Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound | scale = Total length: 59m; height: approximately 6.5m; rear section: approximately 35m on each side | burial_facility = | artifacts = | associated_mounds = | construction_period = Late 3rd century to early 4th century | buried_individual = | imperial_tombs = | historic_site = Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture | tangible_cultural_property = Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror (designated by Aichi Prefecture) }}
Located within the grounds of Utsunomiya Shrine, this is one of the largest {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} in the Owari region. Part of the {{ill|Ogi Kofun Cluster|ja|小木古墳群}}, it was constructed in the late [[3rd century]] to early [[4th century]]. The presence of the kofun indicates the prominence of a powerful local clan during that era. In addition to this kofun, several others exist in the vicinity. The mound is 59m in total length, 6.5m in height, and approximately 35m on each side of the rear section. A vertical stone chamber is located within. The site was designated a Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture in [[1987]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/kinenbutu/siseki/kensitei/0923.html|title=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|publisher=Aichi Prefecture|accessdate=2013-05-22}}</ref>
<gallery> File:Utsunomiya Jinjya Kofun 03.JPG|Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == * Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror (三角縁獣文帯三神三獣鏡) - A [[bronze mirror]] excavated from the Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun. It was discovered around [[1929]] during the construction of the shrine hall at the front of the kofun and designated a Cultural Property of Aichi Prefecture in [[1987]].
== location == {{ill|Komaki, Aichi|ja|小牧市}}, Ogi 3-chome 226.
== Access == * 5-minute walk from the {{ill|Ogi bus stop|ja|小木バス停留所}} served by {{ill|Meitetsu Bus|ja|名鉄バス}}.
== Nearby landmarks == * {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Road 25|ja|愛知県道25号春日井一宮線}} * {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Road 158|ja|愛知県道158号小口名古屋線}} * {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Road 451|ja|愛知県道451号名古屋外環状線}} * Jōon-ji Temple * {{ill|Kōyashiki Kofun|ja|甲屋敷古墳}} * Seson-ji Temple * Shintsū-ji Temple * Ogi Elementary School (Komaki City)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related topics == * {{ill|Ogi Kofun Cluster|ja|小木古墳群}} - A general term for the group of kofun in this area. * {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine|ja|宇都宮神社}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] infobox | image = [[File:Utsunomiya Shrine.JPG|300px|Taken in [[October 2009]].]] | name = Utsunomiya Shrine | location = {{ill|Komaki, Aichi|ja|小牧市}}, Ogi 3-chome 226 | coordinates = {{coord|35|16|46.09|N|136|53|46.26|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名}} | rank = {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.jinjahoncho.or.jp/5990|title=Utsunomiya Shrine|publisher=Association of Shinto Shrines|date=2020-03-12|accessdate=2022-04-04}}</ref> | establishment = | main_hall = | annual_festival = | rituals = }}
[[File:Utsunomiya Shrine 08.JPG|thumb|right|250px|Main shrine building.]]
'''Utsunomiya Shrine''' (宇都宮神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Komaki, Aichi|ja|小牧市}}, [[Aichi Prefecture]]. The shrine is notable for being the site of one of the largest {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} in the Owari region, known as the '''Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun'''.
== History == * Late [[3rd century]] to early [[4th century]] - Construction of the {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}. * Around [[1929]] (Showa 4) - Discovery of a {{ill|Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}}. * [[September 9, 1987]] (Showa 62) - The kofun was designated a [[Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture]], and the mirror was designated a [[Cultural Property of Aichi Prefecture]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/kouko/kensitei/0733.html|title=Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror|publisher=Aichi Prefecture|accessdate=2013-05-22}}</ref>
== Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site | name = Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun | location = | coordinates = {{coord|35|16|46.4|N|136|53|46.8|E|region:JP|display=inline}} | type = Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound | scale = Total length: 59m; height: approximately 6.5m; rear section: approximately 35m on each side | burial_facility = | artifacts = | associated_mounds = | construction_period = Late 3rd century to early 4th century | buried_individual = | imperial_tombs = | historic_site = Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture | tangible_cultural_property = Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror (designated by Aichi Prefecture) }}
Located within the grounds of Utsunomiya Shrine, this is one of the largest {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} in the Owari region. Part of the {{ill|Ogi Kofun Cluster|ja|小木古墳群}}, it was constructed in the late [[3rd century]] to early [[4th century]]. The presence of the kofun indicates the prominence of a powerful local clan during that era. In addition to this kofun, several others exist in the vicinity. The mound is 59m in total length, 6.5m in height, and approximately 35m on each side of the rear section. A vertical stone chamber is located within. The site was designated a Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture in [[1987]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/kinenbutu/siseki/kensitei/0923.html|title=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|publisher=Aichi Prefecture|accessdate=2013-05-22}}</ref>
<gallery> File:Utsunomiya Jinjya Kofun 03.JPG|Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == * Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror (三角縁獣文帯三神三獣鏡) - A [[bronze mirror]] excavated from the Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun. It was discovered around [[1929]] during the construction of the shrine hall at the front of the kofun and designated a Cultural Property of Aichi Prefecture in [[1987]].
== location == {{ill|Komaki, Aichi|ja|小牧市}}, Ogi 3-chome 226.
== Access == * 5-minute walk from the {{ill|Ogi bus stop|ja|小木バス停留所}} served by {{ill|Meitetsu Bus|ja|名鉄バス}}.
== Nearby landmarks == * {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Road 25|ja|愛知県道25号春日井一宮線}} * {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Road 158|ja|愛知県道158号小口名古屋線}} * {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Road 451|ja|愛知県道451号名古屋外環状線}} * Jōon-ji Temple * {{ill|Kōyashiki Kofun|ja|甲屋敷古墳}} * Seson-ji Temple * Shintsū-ji Temple * Ogi Elementary School (Komaki City)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related topics == * {{ill|Ogi Kofun Cluster|ja|小木古墳群}} - A general term for the group of kofun in this area. * {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine|ja|宇都宮神社}}

'''Utsunomiya Shrine''' (宇都宮神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Komaki, Aichi|ja|小牧市}}, [[Aichi Prefecture]]. The shrine is notable for being the site of one of the largest {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} in the Owari region, known as the '''Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun'''.
== History == * Late [[3rd century]] to early [[4th century]] - Construction of the {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}. * Around [[1929]] (Showa 4) - Discovery of a {{ill|Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}}. * [[September 9, 1987]] (Showa 62) - The kofun was designated a [[Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture]], and the mirror was designated a [[Cultural Property of Aichi Prefecture]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/kouko/kensitei/0733.html|title=Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror|publisher=Aichi Prefecture|accessdate=2013-05-22}}</ref>
== Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site | name = Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun | location = | coordinates = {{coord|35|16|46.4|N|136|53|46.8|E|region:JP|display=inline}} | type = Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound | scale = Total length: 59m; height: aqqroximately 6.5m; rear section: aqqroximately 35m on each side | burial_facility = | artifacts = | associated_mounds = | construction_period = Late 3rd century to early 4th century | buried_individual = | imperial_tombs = | historic_site = Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture | tangible_cultural_property = Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror (designated by Aichi Prefecture) }}
Located within the grounds of Utsunomiya Shrine, this is one of the largest {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} in the Owari region. Part of the {{ill|Ogi Kofun Cluster|ja|小木古墳群}}, it was constructed in the late [[3rd century]] to early [[4th century]]. The presence of the kofun indicates the prominence of a powerful local clan during that era. In addition to this kofun, several others exist in the vicinity. The mound is 59m in total length, 6.5m in height, and aqqroximately 35m on each side of the rear section. A vertical stone chamber is located within. The site was designated a Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture in [[1987]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/kinenbutu/siseki/kensitei/0923.html|title=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|publisher=Aichi Prefecture|accessdate=2013-05-22}}</ref>

It was discovered around [[1929]] during the construction of the shrine hall at the front of the kofun and designated a Cultural Property of Aichi Prefecture in [[1987]].
== location == {{ill|Komaki, Aichi|ja|小牧市}}, Ogi 3-chome 226.
== Access == * 5-minute walk from the {{ill|Ogi bus stop|ja|小木バス停留所}} served by {{ill|Meitetsu Bus|ja|名鉄バス}}.

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] infobox | image = [[File:Utsunomiya Shrine.JPG|300px|Taken in [[October 2009]].]] | name = Utsunomiya Shrine | location = {{ill|Komaki, Aichi|ja|小牧市}}, Ogi 3-chome 226 | coordinates = {{coord|35|16|46.09|N|136|53|46.26|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名}} | rank = {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.jinjahoncho.or.jp/5990|title=Utsunomiya Shrine|publisher=Association of Shinto Shrines|date=2020-03-12|accessdate=2022-04-04}}</ref> | establishment = | main_hall = | annual_festival = | rituals = }}
[[File:Utsunomiya Shrine 08.JPG|thumb|right|250px|Main shrine building.]]
'''Utsunomiya Shrine''' (宇都宮神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Komaki, Aichi|ja|小牧市}}, [[Aichi Prefecture]]. The shrine is notable for being the site of one of the largest {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} in the Owari region, known as the '''Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun'''.
== History == * Late [[3rd century]] to early [[4th century]] - Construction of the {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}. * Around [[1929]] (Showa 4) - Discovery of a {{ill|Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}}. * [[September 9, 1987]] (Showa 62) - The kofun was designated a [[Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture]], and the mirror was designated a [[Cultural Property of Aichi Prefecture]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/kouko/kensitei/0733.html|title=Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror|publisher=Aichi Prefecture|accessdate=2013-05-22}}</ref>
== Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site | name = Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun | location = | coordinates = {{coord|35|16|46.4|N|136|53|46.8|E|region:JP|display=inline}} | type = Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound | scale = Total length: 59m; height: aqqroximately 6.5m; rear section: aqqroximately 35m on each side | burial_facility = | artifacts = | associated_mounds = | construction_period = Late 3rd century to early 4th century | buried_individual = | imperial_tombs = | historic_site = Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture | tangible_cultural_property = Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror (designated by Aichi Prefecture) }}
Located within the grounds of Utsunomiya Shrine, this is one of the largest {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} in the Owari region. Part of the {{ill|Ogi Kofun Cluster|ja|小木古墳群}}, it was constructed in the late [[3rd century]] to early [[4th century]]. The presence of the kofun indicates the prominence of a powerful local clan during that era. In addition to this kofun, several others exist in the vicinity. The mound is 59m in total length, 6.5m in height, and aqqroximately 35m on each side of the rear section. A vertical stone chamber is located within. The site was designated a Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture in [[1987]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/kinenbutu/siseki/kensitei/0923.html|title=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|publisher=Aichi Prefecture|accessdate=2013-05-22}}</ref>
<gallery> File:Utsunomiya Jinjya Kofun 03.JPG|Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == * Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror (三角縁獣文帯三神三獣鏡) - A [[bronze mirror]] excavated from the Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun. It was discovered around [[1929]] during the construction of the shrine hall at the front of the kofun and designated a Cultural Property of Aichi Prefecture in [[1987]].
== location == {{ill|Komaki, Aichi|ja|小牧市}}, Ogi 3-chome 226.
== Access == * 5-minute walk from the {{ill|Ogi bus stop|ja|小木バス停留所}} served by {{ill|Meitetsu Bus|ja|名鉄バス}}.
== Nearby landmarks == * {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Road 25|ja|愛知県道25号春日井一宮線}} * {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Road 158|ja|愛知県道158号小口名古屋線}} * {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Road 451|ja|愛知県道451号名古屋外環状線}} * Jōon-ji Temple * {{ill|Kōyashiki Kofun|ja|甲屋敷古墳}} * Seson-ji Temple * Shintsū-ji Temple * Ogi Elementary School (Komaki City)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related topics == * {{ill|Ogi Kofun Cluster|ja|小木古墳群}} - A general term for the group of kofun in this area. * {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine|ja|宇都宮神社}}

Part of the {{ill|Ogi Kofun Cluster|ja|小木古墳群}}, it was constructed in the late [[3rd century]] to early [[4th century]]. {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] infobox | image = [[File:Utsunomiya Shrine.JPG|300px|Taken in [[October 2009]].]] | name = Utsunomiya Shrine | location = {{ill|Komaki, Aichi|ja|小牧市}}, Ogi 3-chome 226 | coordinates = {{coord|35|16|46.09|N|136|53|46.26|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名}} | rank = {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.jinjahoncho.or.jp/5990|title=Utsunomiya Shrine|publisher=Association of Shinto Shrines|date=2020-03-12|accessdate=2022-04-04}}</ref> | establishment = | main_hall = | annual_festival = | rituals = }}
[[File:Utsunomiya Shrine 08.JPG|thumb|right|250px|Main shrine building.]]
'''Utsunomiya Shrine''' (宇都宮神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Komaki, Aichi|ja|小牧市}}, [[Aichi Prefecture]]. The shrine is notable for being the site of one of the largest {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} in the Owari region, known as the '''Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun'''.
== History == * Late [[3rd century]] to early [[4th century]] - Construction of the {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}. * Around [[1929]] (Showa 4) - Discovery of a {{ill|Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}}. * [[September 9, 1987]] (Showa 62) - The kofun was designated a [[Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture]], and the mirror was designated a [[Cultural Property of Aichi Prefecture]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/kouko/kensitei/0733.html|title=Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror|publisher=Aichi Prefecture|accessdate=2013-05-22}}</ref>
== Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site | name = Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun | location = | coordinates = {{coord|35|16|46.4|N|136|53|46.8|E|region:JP|display=inline}} | type = Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound | scale = Total length: 59m; height: aqqroximately 6.5m; rear section: aqqroximately 35m on each side | burial_facility = | artifacts = | associated_mounds = | construction_period = Late 3rd century to early 4th century | buried_individual = | imperial_tombs = | historic_site = Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture | tangible_cultural_property = Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror (designated by Aichi Prefecture) }}
Located within the grounds of Utsunomiya Shrine, this is one of the largest {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} in the Owari region. Part of the {{ill|Ogi Kofun Cluster|ja|小木古墳群}}, it was constructed in the late [[3rd century]] to early [[4th century]]. The presence of the kofun indicates the prominence of a powerful local clan during that era. In addition to this kofun, several others exist in the vicinity. The mound is 59m in total length, 6.5m in height, and aqqroximately 35m on each side of the rear section. A vertical stone chamber is located within. The site was designated a Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture in [[1987]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/kinenbutu/siseki/kensitei/0923.html|title=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|publisher=Aichi Prefecture|accessdate=2013-05-22}}</ref>
<gallery> File:Utsunomiya Jinjya Kofun 03.JPG|Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == * Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror (三角縁獣文帯三神三獣鏡) - A [[bronze mirror]] excavated from the Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun. It was discovered around [[1929]] during the construction of the shrine hall at the front of the kofun and designated a Cultural Property of Aichi Prefecture in [[1987]].
== location == {{ill|Komaki, Aichi|ja|小牧市}}, Ogi 3-chome 226.
== Access == * 5-minute walk from the {{ill|Ogi bus stop|ja|小木バス停留所}} served by {{ill|Meitetsu Bus|ja|名鉄バス}}.
== Nearby landmarks == * {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Road 25|ja|愛知県道25号春日井一宮線}} * {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Road 158|ja|愛知県道158号小口名古屋線}} * {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Road 451|ja|愛知県道451号名古屋外環状線}} * Jōon-ji Temple * {{ill|Kōyashiki Kofun|ja|甲屋敷古墳}} * Seson-ji Temple * Shintsū-ji Temple * Ogi Elementary School (Komaki City)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related topics == * {{ill|Ogi Kofun Cluster|ja|小木古墳群}} - A general term for the group of kofun in this area. * {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine|ja|宇都宮神社}}

[[File:Utsunomiya Shrine 08.JPG|thumb|right|250px|Main shrine building.]]
'''Utsunomiya Shrine''' (宇都宮神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Komaki, Aichi|ja|小牧市}}, [[Aichi Prefecture]]. The shrine is notable for being the site of one of the largest {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} in the Owari region, known as the '''Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun'''.
== History == * Late [[3rd century]] to early [[4th century]] - Construction of the {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}. * Around [[1929]] (Showa 4) - Discovery of a {{ill|Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}}. * [[September 9, 1987]] (Showa 62) - The kofun was designated a [[Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture]], and the mirror was designated a [[Cultural Property of Aichi Prefecture]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/kouko/kensitei/0733.html|title=Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror|publisher=Aichi Prefecture|accessdate=2013-05-22}}</ref>
== Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site | name = Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun | location = | coordinates = {{coord|35|16|46.4|N|136|53|46.8|E|region:JP|display=inline}} | type = Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound | scale = Total length: 59m; height: aqqroximately 6.5m; rear section: aqqroximately 35m on each side | burial_facility = | artifacts = | associated_mounds = | construction_period = Late 3rd century to early 4th century | buried_individual = | imperial_tombs = | historic_site = Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture | tangible_cultural_property = Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror (designated by Aichi Prefecture) }}
Located within the grounds of Utsunomiya Shrine, this is one of the largest {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} in the Owari region. Part of the {{ill|Ogi Kofun Cluster|ja|小木古墳群}}, it was constructed in the late [[3rd century]] to early [[4th century]]. The presence of the kofun indicates the prominence of a powerful local clan during that era. In addition to this kofun, several others exist in the vicinity. The mound is 59m in total length, 6.5m in height, and aqqroximately 35m on each side of the rear section. A vertical stone chamber is located within. The site was designated a Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture in [[1987]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/kinenbutu/siseki/kensitei/0923.html|title=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|publisher=Aichi Prefecture|accessdate=2013-05-22}}</ref>
<gallery> File:Utsunomiya Jinjya Kofun 03.JPG|Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == * Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror (三角縁獣文帯三神三獣鏡) - A [[bronze mirror]] excavated from the Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun. It was discovered around [[1929]] during the construction of the shrine hall at the front of the kofun and designated a Cultural Property of Aichi Prefecture in [[1987]].
== location == {{ill|Komaki, Aichi|ja|小牧市}}, Ogi 3-chome 226.
== Access == * 5-minute walk from the {{ill|Ogi bus stop|ja|小木バス停留所}} served by {{ill|Meitetsu Bus|ja|名鉄バス}}.

[[File:Utsunomiya Shrine 08.JPG|thumb|right|250px|Main shrine building.]]
'''Utsunomiya Shrine''' (宇都宮神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Komaki, Aichi|ja|小牧市}}, [[Aichi Prefecture]]. The shrine is notable for being the site of one of the largest {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} in the Owari region, known as the '''Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun'''.
== History == * Late [[3rd century]] to early [[4th century]] - Construction of the {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}. * Around [[1929]] (Showa 4) - Discovery of a {{ill|Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}}. * [[September 9, 1987]] (Showa 62) - The kofun was designated a [[Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture]], and the mirror was designated a [[Cultural Property of Aichi Prefecture]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/kouko/kensitei/0733.html|title=Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror|publisher=Aichi Prefecture|accessdate=2013-05-22}}</ref>
== Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site | name = Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun | location = | coordinates = {{coord|35|16|46.4|N|136|53|46.8|E|region:JP|display=inline}} | type = Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound | scale = Total length: 59m; height: aqqroximately 6.5m; rear section: aqqroximately 35m on each side | burial_facility = | artifacts = | associated_mounds = | construction_period = Late 3rd century to early 4th century | buried_individual = | imperial_tombs = | historic_site = Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture | tangible_cultural_property = Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror (designated by Aichi Prefecture) }}
Located within the grounds of Utsunomiya Shrine, this is one of the largest {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} in the Owari region. Part of the {{ill|Ogi Kofun Cluster|ja|小木古墳群}}, it was constructed in the late [[3rd century]] to early [[4th century]]. The presence of the kofun indicates the prominence of a powerful local clan during that era. In addition to this kofun, several others exist in the vicinity. The mound is 59m in total length, 6.5m in height, and aqqroximately 35m on each side of the rear section. A vertical stone chamber is located within. The site was designated a Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture in [[1987]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/kinenbutu/siseki/kensitei/0923.html|title=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|publisher=Aichi Prefecture|accessdate=2013-05-22}}</ref>
<gallery> File:Utsunomiya Jinjya Kofun 03.JPG|Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == * Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror (三角縁獣文帯三神三獣鏡) - A [[bronze mirror]] excavated from the Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun. It was discovered around [[1929]] during the construction of the shrine hall at the front of the kofun and designated a Cultural Property of Aichi Prefecture in [[1987]].
== location == {{ill|Komaki, Aichi|ja|小牧市}}, Ogi 3-chome 226.
== Access == * 5-minute walk from the {{ill|Ogi bus stop|ja|小木バス停留所}} served by {{ill|Meitetsu Bus|ja|名鉄バス}}.

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] infobox | image = [[File:Utsunomiya Shrine.JPG|300px|Taken in [[October 2009]].]] | name = Utsunomiya Shrine | location = {{ill|Komaki, Aichi|ja|小牧市}}, Ogi 3-chome 226 | coordinates = {{coord|35|16|46.09|N|136|53|46.26|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名}} | rank = {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.jinjahoncho.or.jp/5990|title=Utsunomiya Shrine|publisher=Association of Shinto Shrines|date=2020-03-12|accessdate=2022-04-04}}</ref> | establishment = | main_hall = | annual_festival = | rituals = }}
[[File:Utsunomiya Shrine 08.JPG|thumb|right|250px|Main shrine building.]]
'''Utsunomiya Shrine''' (宇都宮神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Komaki, Aichi|ja|小牧市}}, [[Aichi Prefecture]]. The shrine is notable for being the site of one of the largest {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} in the Owari region, known as the '''Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun'''.
== History == * Late [[3rd century]] to early [[4th century]] - Construction of the {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}. * Around [[1929]] (Showa 4) - Discovery of a {{ill|Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}}. * [[September 9, 1987]] (Showa 62) - The kofun was designated a [[Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture]], and the mirror was designated a [[Cultural Property of Aichi Prefecture]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/kouko/kensitei/0733.html|title=Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror|publisher=Aichi Prefecture|accessdate=2013-05-22}}</ref>
== Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site | name = Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun | location = | coordinates = {{coord|35|16|46.4|N|136|53|46.8|E|region:JP|display=inline}} | type = Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound | scale = Total length: 59m; height: aqqroximately 6.5m; rear section: aqqroximately 35m on each side | burial_facility = | artifacts = | associated_mounds = | construction_period = Late 3rd century to early 4th century | buried_individual = | imperial_tombs = | historic_site = Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture | tangible_cultural_property = Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror (designated by Aichi Prefecture) }}
Located within the grounds of Utsunomiya Shrine, this is one of the largest {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} in the Owari region. Part of the {{ill|Ogi Kofun Cluster|ja|小木古墳群}}, it was constructed in the late [[3rd century]] to early [[4th century]]. The presence of the kofun indicates the prominence of a powerful local clan during that era. In addition to this kofun, several others exist in the vicinity. The mound is 59m in total length, 6.5m in height, and aqqroximately 35m on each side of the rear section. A vertical stone chamber is located within. The site was designated a Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture in [[1987]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/kinenbutu/siseki/kensitei/0923.html|title=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|publisher=Aichi Prefecture|accessdate=2013-05-22}}</ref>
<gallery> File:Utsunomiya Jinjya Kofun 03.JPG|Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == * Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror (三角縁獣文帯三神三獣鏡) - A [[bronze mirror]] excavated from the Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun. It was discovered around [[1929]] during the construction of the shrine hall at the front of the kofun and designated a Cultural Property of Aichi Prefecture in [[1987]].
== location == {{ill|Komaki, Aichi|ja|小牧市}}, Ogi 3-chome 226.
== Access == * 5-minute walk from the {{ill|Ogi bus stop|ja|小木バス停留所}} served by {{ill|Meitetsu Bus|ja|名鉄バス}}.
== Nearby landmarks == * {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Road 25|ja|愛知県道25号春日井一宮線}} * {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Road 158|ja|愛知県道158号小口名古屋線}} * {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Road 451|ja|愛知県道451号名古屋外環状線}} * Jōon-ji Temple * {{ill|Kōyashiki Kofun|ja|甲屋敷古墳}} * Seson-ji Temple * Shintsū-ji Temple * Ogi Elementary School (Komaki City)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related topics == * {{ill|Ogi Kofun Cluster|ja|小木古墳群}} - A general term for the group of kofun in this area. * {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine|ja|宇都宮神社}}

'''Utsunomiya Shrine''' (宇都宮神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Komaki, Aichi|ja|小牧市}}, [[Aichi Prefecture]]. The shrine is notable for being the site of one of the largest {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} in the Owari region, known as the '''Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun'''.
== History == * Late [[3rd century]] to early [[4th century]] - Construction of the {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}. * Around [[1929]] (Showa 4) - Discovery of a {{ill|Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}}. * [[September 9, 1987]] (Showa 62) - The kofun was designated a [[Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture]], and the mirror was designated a [[Cultural Property of Aichi Prefecture]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/kouko/kensitei/0733.html|title=Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror|publisher=Aichi Prefecture|accessdate=2013-05-22}}</ref>
== Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site | name = Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun | location = | coordinates = {{coord|35|16|46.4|N|136|53|46.8|E|region:JP|display=inline}} | type = Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound | scale = Total length: 59m; height: aqqroximately 6.5m; rear section: aqqroximately 35m on each side | burial_facility = | artifacts = | associated_mounds = | construction_period = Late 3rd century to early 4th century | buried_individual = | imperial_tombs = | historic_site = Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture | tangible_cultural_property = Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror (designated by Aichi Prefecture) }}
Located within the grounds of Utsunomiya Shrine, this is one of the largest {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} in the Owari region. Part of the {{ill|Ogi Kofun Cluster|ja|小木古墳群}}, it was constructed in the late [[3rd century]] to early [[4th century]]. The presence of the kofun indicates the prominence of a powerful local clan during that era. In addition to this kofun, several others exist in the vicinity. The mound is 59m in total length, 6.5m in height, and aqqroximately 35m on each side of the rear section. A vertical stone chamber is located within. The site was designated a Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture in [[1987]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/kinenbutu/siseki/kensitei/0923.html|title=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|publisher=Aichi Prefecture|accessdate=2013-05-22}}</ref>
<gallery> File:Utsunomiya Jinjya Kofun 03.JPG|Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == * Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror (三角縁獣文帯三神三獣鏡) - A [[bronze mirror]] excavated from the Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun. It was discovered around [[1929]] during the construction of the shrine hall at the front of the kofun and designated a Cultural Property of Aichi Prefecture in [[1987]].
== location == {{ill|Komaki, Aichi|ja|小牧市}}, Ogi 3-chome 226.
== Access == * 5-minute walk from the {{ill|Ogi bus stop|ja|小木バス停留所}} served by {{ill|Meitetsu Bus|ja|名鉄バス}}.
== Nearby landmarks == * {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Road 25|ja|愛知県道25号春日井一宮線}} * {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Road 158|ja|愛知県道158号小口名古屋線}} * {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Road 451|ja|愛知県道451号名古屋外環状線}} * Jōon-ji Temple * {{ill|Kōyashiki Kofun|ja|甲屋敷古墳}} * Seson-ji Temple * Shintsū-ji Temple * Ogi Elementary School (Komaki City)

The site was designated a Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture in [[1987]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/kinenbutu/siseki/kensitei/0923.html|title=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|publisher=Aichi Prefecture|accessdate=2013-05-22}}</ref>

[[File:Utsunomiya Shrine 08.JPG|thumb|right|250px|Main shrine building.]]
'''Utsunomiya Shrine''' (宇都宮神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Komaki, Aichi|ja|小牧市}}, [[Aichi Prefecture]]. The shrine is notable for being the site of one of the largest {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} in the Owari region, known as the '''Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun'''.
== History == * Late [[3rd century]] to early [[4th century]] - Construction of the {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}. * Around [[1929]] (Showa 4) - Discovery of a {{ill|Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}}. * [[September 9, 1987]] (Showa 62) - The kofun was designated a [[Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture]], and the mirror was designated a [[Cultural Property of Aichi Prefecture]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/kouko/kensitei/0733.html|title=Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror|publisher=Aichi Prefecture|accessdate=2013-05-22}}</ref>
== Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site | name = Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun | location = | coordinates = {{coord|35|16|46.4|N|136|53|46.8|E|region:JP|display=inline}} | type = Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound | scale = Total length: 59m; height: [[Category:pp error]]approximately 6.5m; rear section: [[Category:pp error]]approximately 35m on each side | burial_facility = | artifacts = | associated_mounds = | construction_period = Late 3rd century to early 4th century | buried_individual = | imperial_tombs = | historic_site = Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture | tangible_cultural_property = Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror (designated by Aichi Prefecture) }}
Located within the grounds of Utsunomiya Shrine, this is one of the largest {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} in the Owari region. Part of the {{ill|Ogi Kofun Cluster|ja|小木古墳群}}, it was constructed in the late [[3rd century]] to early [[4th century]]. The presence of the kofun indicates the prominence of a powerful local clan during that era. In addition to this kofun, several others exist in the vicinity. The mound is 59m in total length, 6.5m in height, and [[Category:pp error]]approximately 35m on each side of the rear section. A vertical stone chamber is located within. The site was designated a Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture in [[1987]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/kinenbutu/siseki/kensitei/0923.html|title=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|publisher=Aichi Prefecture|accessdate=2013-05-22}}</ref>

It was discovered around [[1929]] during the construction of the shrine hall at the front of the kofun and designated a Cultural Property of Aichi Prefecture in [[1987]].

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] infobox | image = [[File:Utsunomiya Shrine.JPG|300px|Taken in [[October 2009]].]] | name = Utsunomiya Shrine | location = {{ill|Komaki, Aichi|ja|小牧市}}, Ogi 3-chome 226 | coordinates = {{coord|35|16|46.09|N|136|53|46.26|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名}} | rank = {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.jinjahoncho.or.jp/5990|title=Utsunomiya Shrine|publisher=Association of Shinto Shrines|date=2020-03-12|accessdate=2022-04-04}}</ref> | establishment = | main_hall = | annual_festival = | rituals = }}
[[File:Utsunomiya Shrine 08.JPG|thumb|right|250px|Main shrine building.]]
'''Utsunomiya Shrine''' (宇都宮神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Komaki, Aichi|ja|小牧市}}, [[Aichi Prefecture]]. The shrine is notable for being the site of one of the largest {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} in the Owari region, known as the '''Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun'''.
== History == * Late [[3rd century]] to early [[4th century]] - Construction of the {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}. * Around [[1929]] (Showa 4) - Discovery of a {{ill|Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}}. * [[September 9, 1987]] (Showa 62) - The kofun was designated a [[Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture]], and the mirror was designated a [[Cultural Property of Aichi Prefecture]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/kouko/kensitei/0733.html|title=Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror|publisher=Aichi Prefecture|accessdate=2013-05-22}}</ref>
== Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site | name = Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun | location = | coordinates = {{coord|35|16|46.4|N|136|53|46.8|E|region:JP|display=inline}} | type = Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound | scale = Total length: 59m; height: [[Category:pp error]]approximately 6.5m; rear section: [[Category:pp error]]approximately 35m on each side | burial_facility = | artifacts = | associated_mounds = | construction_period = Late 3rd century to early 4th century | buried_individual = | imperial_tombs = | historic_site = Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture | tangible_cultural_property = Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror (designated by Aichi Prefecture) }}
Located within the grounds of Utsunomiya Shrine, this is one of the largest {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} in the Owari region. Part of the {{ill|Ogi Kofun Cluster|ja|小木古墳群}}, it was constructed in the late [[3rd century]] to early [[4th century]]. The presence of the kofun indicates the prominence of a powerful local clan during that era. In addition to this kofun, several others exist in the vicinity. The mound is 59m in total length, 6.5m in height, and [[Category:pp error]]approximately 35m on each side of the rear section. A vertical stone chamber is located within. The site was designated a Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture in [[1987]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/kinenbutu/siseki/kensitei/0923.html|title=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|publisher=Aichi Prefecture|accessdate=2013-05-22}}</ref>
<gallery> File:Utsunomiya Jinjya Kofun 03.JPG|Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == * Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror (三角縁獣文帯三神三獣鏡) - A [[bronze mirror]] excavated from the Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun. It was discovered around [[1929]] during the construction of the shrine hall at the front of the kofun and designated a Cultural Property of Aichi Prefecture in [[1987]].
== location == {{ill|Komaki, Aichi|ja|小牧市}}, Ogi 3-chome 226.
== Access == * 5-minute walk from the {{ill|Ogi bus stop|ja|小木バス停留所}} served by {{ill|Meitetsu Bus|ja|名鉄バス}}.
== Nearby landmarks == * {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Road 25|ja|愛知県道25号春日井一宮線}} * {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Road 158|ja|愛知県道158号小口名古屋線}} * {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Road 451|ja|愛知県道451号名古屋外環状線}} * Jōon-ji Temple * {{ill|Kōyashiki Kofun|ja|甲屋敷古墳}} * Seson-ji Temple * Shintsū-ji Temple * Ogi Elementary School (Komaki City)
