'''Utsunomiya Shrine''' (宇都宮神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Komaki|Komaki, Aichi]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. The shrine is notable for being the site of one of the largest {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in the Owari region, known as the '''Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun'''.

== History ==
* Late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]] to early [[:en:4th century|4th century]] - built of the {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}.
* Around [[:en:1929|1929]] (Showa 4) - Discovery of a {{ill|Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}.
* [[September 9, 1987]] (Showa 62) - The kofun was Historic Site1 a [[Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture]], and the mirror was Historic Site1 a [[designation1 of Aichi Prefecture]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/kouko/kensitei/0733.html|title=Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror|publisher=Aichi Prefecture|accessdate=2013-05-22}}</ref>

== Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun ==
{{Infobox ancient site
| name = Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun
| location = 
| coordinates = {{coord|35|16|46.4|N|136|53|46.8|E|region:JP|display=inline}}
| type = Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound
| map_size = Total length: 59m; height: approximately 6.5m; rear section: approximately 35m on each side
| Burial Facility = 
| burial goods = 
| associated_mounds = 
| built = Late 3rd century to early 4th century
| Interred_individual = 
| imperial_tombs = 
| historic_site = Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture
| tangible_notes = Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror (Historic Site1 by Aichi Prefecture)
}}

Located within the grounds of Utsunomiya Shrine, this is one of the largest {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in the Owari region. Part of the {{ill|Koki Kofun Cluster|ja|小木古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koki Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, it was constructed in the late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]] to early [[:en:4th century|4th century]]. The presence of the kofun indicates the prominence of a powerful local clan during that era. In addition to this kofun, several others exist in the vicinity. The mound is 59m in total length, 6.5m in height, and approximately 35m on each side of the rear section. A vertical stone chamber is located within. The site was Historic Site1 a Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture in [[:en:1987|1987]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/kinenbutu/siseki/kensitei/0923.html|title=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|publisher=Aichi Prefecture|accessdate=2013-05-22}}</ref>

</gallery>

It was discovered around [[:en:1929|1929]] during the construction of the shrine hall at the front of the kofun and Historic Site1 a designation1 of Aichi Prefecture in [[:en:1987|1987]].

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] infobox | image = [[File:Utsunomiya Shrine.JPG|300px|Taken in [[:en:October 2009|October 2009]].]] | name = Utsunomiya Shrine | location = [[:en:Komaki|Komaki, Aichi]], Ogi 3-chome 226 | coordinates = {{coord|35|16|46.09|N|136|53|46.26|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | deities = [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]], [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} | Rank = {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.jinjahoncho.or.jp/5990|title=Utsunomiya Shrine|publisher=Association of Shinto Shrines|date=2020-03-12|accessdate=2022-04-04}}</ref> | establishment = | main_hall = | annual_festival = | rituals = }}
[[File:Utsunomiya Shrine 08.JPG|thumb|right|250px|Main shrine building.]]
'''Utsunomiya Shrine''' (宇都宮神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Komaki|Komaki, Aichi]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. The shrine is notable for being the site of one of the largest {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in the Owari region, known as the '''Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun'''.
== History == * Late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]] to early [[:en:4th century|4th century]] - built of the {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. * Around [[:en:1929|1929]] (Showa 4) - Discovery of a {{ill|Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}. * [[September 9, 1987]] (Showa 62) - The kofun was Historic Site1 a [[Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture]], and the mirror was Historic Site1 a [[designation1 of Aichi Prefecture]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/kouko/kensitei/0733.html|title=Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror|publisher=Aichi Prefecture|accessdate=2013-05-22}}</ref>
== Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site | name = Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun | location = | coordinates = {{coord|35|16|46.4|N|136|53|46.8|E|region:JP|display=inline}} | type = Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound | map_size = Total length: 59m; height: approximately 6.5m; rear section: approximately 35m on each side | Burial Facility = | burial goods = | associated_mounds = | built = Late 3rd century to early 4th century | Interred_individual = | imperial_tombs = | historic_site = Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture | tangible_notes = Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror (Historic Site1 by Aichi Prefecture) }}
Located within the grounds of Utsunomiya Shrine, this is one of the largest {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in the Owari region. Part of the {{ill|Koki Kofun Cluster|ja|小木古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koki Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, it was constructed in the late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]] to early [[:en:4th century|4th century]]. The presence of the kofun indicates the prominence of a powerful local clan during that era. In addition to this kofun, several others exist in the vicinity. The mound is 59m in total length, 6.5m in height, and approximately 35m on each side of the rear section. A vertical stone chamber is located within. The site was Historic Site1 a Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture in [[:en:1987|1987]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/kinenbutu/siseki/kensitei/0923.html|title=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|publisher=Aichi Prefecture|accessdate=2013-05-22}}</ref>
<gallery> File:Utsunomiya Jinjya Kofun 03.JPG|Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun </gallery>
== Historic Site == * Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror (三角縁獣文帯三神三獣鏡) - A [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]] excavated from the Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun. It was discovered around [[:en:1929|1929]] during the construction of the shrine hall at the front of the kofun and Historic Site1 a designation1 of Aichi Prefecture in [[:en:1987|1987]].
== location == [[:en:Komaki|Komaki, Aichi]], Ogi 3-chome 226.
== Access == * 5-minute walk from the {{ill|Ogi bus stop|ja|小木バス停留所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogi bus stop|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} served by [[:en:Meitetsu Bus|Meitetsu Bus]].
== Nearby landmarks == * {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Road 25|ja|愛知県道25号春日井一宮線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefectural Road 25|1=Aichi Prefectural Road Route 25|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11494509}} * {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Road 158|ja|愛知県道158号小口名古屋線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefectural Road 158|1=Aichi Prefectural Road Route 158|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11494413}} * {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Road 451|ja|愛知県道451号名古屋外環状線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefectural Road 451|1=Aichi Prefectural Road Route 451|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11494718}} * Jōon-ji Temple * {{ill|Kōyashiki Kofun|ja|甲屋敷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōyashiki Kofun|qq=}} * Seson-ji Temple * Shintsū-ji Temple * Ogi Elementary School (Komaki City)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Koki Kofun Cluster|ja|小木古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koki Kofun Cluster|qq=}} - A general term for the group of kofun in this area. * {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine|ja|宇都宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shrine|qq=}}

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] infobox | image = [[File:Utsunomiya Shrine.JPG|300px|Taken in [[:en:October 2009|October 2009]].]] | name = Utsunomiya Shrine | location = [[:en:Komaki|Komaki, Aichi]], Ogi 3-chome 226 | coordinates = {{coord|35|16|46.09|N|136|53|46.26|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | deities = [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]], [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} | Rank = {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.jinjahoncho.or.jp/5990|title=Utsunomiya Shrine|publisher=Association of Shinto Shrines|date=2020-03-12|accessdate=2022-04-04}}</ref> | establishment = | main_hall = | annual_festival = | rituals = }}
[[File:Utsunomiya Shrine 08.JPG|thumb|right|250px|Main shrine building.]]
'''Utsunomiya Shrine''' (宇都宮神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Komaki|Komaki, Aichi]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. The shrine is notable for being the site of one of the largest {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in the Owari region, known as the '''Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun'''.
== History == * Late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]] to early [[:en:4th century|4th century]] - built of the {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. * Around [[:en:1929|1929]] (Showa 4) - Discovery of a {{ill|Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}. * [[September 9, 1987]] (Showa 62) - The kofun was Historic Site1 a [[Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture]], and the mirror was Historic Site1 a [[designation1 of Aichi Prefecture]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/kouko/kensitei/0733.html|title=Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror|publisher=Aichi Prefecture|accessdate=2013-05-22}}</ref>
== Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site | name = Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun | location = | coordinates = {{coord|35|16|46.4|N|136|53|46.8|E|region:JP|display=inline}} | type = Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound | map_size = Total length: 59m; height: approximately 6.5m; rear section: approximately 35m on each side | Burial Facility = | burial goods = | associated_mounds = | built = Late 3rd century to early 4th century | Interred_individual = | imperial_tombs = | historic_site = Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture | tangible_notes = Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror (Historic Site1 by Aichi Prefecture) }}
Located within the grounds of Utsunomiya Shrine, this is one of the largest {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in the Owari region. Part of the {{ill|Koki Kofun Cluster|ja|小木古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koki Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, it was constructed in the late [[:en:3rd century|3rd century]] to early [[:en:4th century|4th century]]. The presence of the kofun indicates the prominence of a powerful local clan during that era. In addition to this kofun, several others exist in the vicinity. The mound is 59m in total length, 6.5m in height, and approximately 35m on each side of the rear section. A vertical stone chamber is located within. The site was Historic Site1 a Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture in [[:en:1987|1987]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/kinenbutu/siseki/kensitei/0923.html|title=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|publisher=Aichi Prefecture|accessdate=2013-05-22}}</ref>
<gallery> File:Utsunomiya Jinjya Kofun 03.JPG|Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun </gallery>
== Historic Site == * Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror (三角縁獣文帯三神三獣鏡) - A [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]] excavated from the Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun. It was discovered around [[:en:1929|1929]] during the construction of the shrine hall at the front of the kofun and Historic Site1 a designation1 of Aichi Prefecture in [[:en:1987|1987]].
== location == [[:en:Komaki|Komaki, Aichi]], Ogi 3-chome 226.
== Access == * 5-minute walk from the {{ill|Ogi bus stop|ja|小木バス停留所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogi bus stop|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} served by [[:en:Meitetsu Bus|Meitetsu Bus]].
== Nearby landmarks == * {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Road 25|ja|愛知県道25号春日井一宮線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefectural Road 25|1=Aichi Prefectural Road Route 25|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11494509}} * {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Road 158|ja|愛知県道158号小口名古屋線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefectural Road 158|1=Aichi Prefectural Road Route 158|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11494413}} * {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Road 451|ja|愛知県道451号名古屋外環状線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefectural Road 451|1=Aichi Prefectural Road Route 451|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11494718}} * Jōon-ji Temple * {{ill|Kōyashiki Kofun|ja|甲屋敷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōyashiki Kofun|qq=}} * Seson-ji Temple * Shintsū-ji Temple * Ogi Elementary School (Komaki City)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Koki Kofun Cluster|ja|小木古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koki Kofun Cluster|qq=}} - A general term for the group of kofun in this area. * {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine|ja|宇都宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shrine|qq=}}
== External links == {{Commonscat|Utsunomiya Shrine}} {{Commonscat|Utsunomiya Jinjya Kofun|宇都宮神社古墳}} * [https://www.utunomiyajinjya-komaki.com/ Utsunomiya Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/2/9111.html Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun (Komaki City Hall)] * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/2/9115.html Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror (Komaki City Hall)] * [https://apec.aichi-c.ed.jp/kyouka/shakai/kyouzai/2018/syakai/owari/owa042.htm Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun (Aichi ACE Net)]

[[File:Utsunomiya Shrine 08.JPG|thumb|right|250px|Main shrine building.]]
'''Utsunomiya Shrine''' (宇都宮神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Komaki|en|Komaki|WD=Q389537|lt=Komaki, Aichi}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture}}. The shrine is notable for being the site of one of the largest {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in the Owari region, known as the '''Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun'''.
== History == * Late {{ill|3rd century|en|3rd century|WD=Q8101|lt=3rd century}} to early {{ill|4th century|en|4th century|WD=Q8099|lt=4th century}} - built of the {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. * Around {{ill|1929|en|1929|WD=Q18792|lt=1929}} (Showa 4) - Discovery of a {{ill|Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}. * [[September 9, 1987]] (Showa 62) - The kofun was Historic Site1 a [[Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture]], and the mirror was Historic Site1 a [[designation1 of Aichi Prefecture]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/kouko/kensitei/0733.html|title=Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror|publisher=Aichi Prefecture|accessdate=2013-05-22}}</ref>
== Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site | name = Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun | location = | coordinates = {{coord|35|16|46.4|N|136|53|46.8|E|region:JP|display=inline}} | type = Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound | map_size = Total length: 59m; height: approximately 6.5m; rear section: approximately 35m on each side | Burial Facility = | burial goods = | associated_mounds = | built = Late 3rd century to early 4th century | Interred_individual = | imperial_tombs = | historic_site = Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture | tangible_notes = Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror (Historic Site1 by Aichi Prefecture) }}
Located within the grounds of Utsunomiya Shrine, this is one of the largest {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in the Owari region. Part of the {{ill|Koki Kofun Cluster|ja|小木古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koki Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, it was constructed in the late {{ill|3rd century|en|3rd century|WD=Q8101|lt=3rd century}} to early {{ill|4th century|en|4th century|WD=Q8099|lt=4th century}}. The presence of the kofun indicates the prominence of a powerful local clan during that era. In addition to this kofun, several others exist in the vicinity. The mound is 59m in total length, 6.5m in height, and approximately 35m on each side of the rear section. A vertical stone chamber is located within. The site was Historic Site1 a Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture in {{ill|1987|en|1987|WD=Q2429|lt=1987}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/kinenbutu/siseki/kensitei/0923.html|title=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|publisher=Aichi Prefecture|accessdate=2013-05-22}}</ref>
<gallery> File:Utsunomiya Jinjya Kofun 03.JPG|Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun </gallery>
== Historic Site == * Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror (三角縁獣文帯三神三獣鏡) - A {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror}} excavated from the Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun. It was discovered around {{ill|1929|en|1929|WD=Q18792|lt=1929}} during the construction of the shrine hall at the front of the kofun and Historic Site1 a designation1 of Aichi Prefecture in {{ill|1987|en|1987|WD=Q2429|lt=1987}}.
== location == {{ill|Komaki|en|Komaki|WD=Q389537|lt=Komaki, Aichi}}, Ogi 3-chome 226.
== Access == * 5-minute walk from the {{ill|Ogi bus stop|ja|小木バス停留所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogi bus stop|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} served by {{ill|Meitetsu Bus|en|Meitetsu Bus|WD=Q6810397|lt=Meitetsu Bus}}.
== Nearby landmarks == * {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Road 25|ja|愛知県道25号春日井一宮線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefectural Road 25|1=Aichi Prefectural Road Route 25|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11494509}} * {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Road 158|ja|愛知県道158号小口名古屋線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefectural Road 158|1=Aichi Prefectural Road Route 158|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11494413}} * {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Road 451|ja|愛知県道451号名古屋外環状線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefectural Road 451|1=Aichi Prefectural Road Route 451|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11494718}} * Jōon-ji Temple * {{ill|Kōyashiki Kofun|ja|甲屋敷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōyashiki Kofun|qq=}} * Seson-ji Temple * Shintsū-ji Temple * Ogi Elementary School (Komaki City)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Koki Kofun Cluster|ja|小木古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koki Kofun Cluster|qq=}} - A general term for the group of kofun in this area. * {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine|ja|宇都宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shrine|qq=}}
== External links == {{Commonscat|Utsunomiya Shrine}} {{Commonscat|Utsunomiya Jinjya Kofun|宇都宮神社古墳}} * [https://www.utunomiyajinjya-komaki.com/ Utsunomiya Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/2/9111.html Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun (Komaki City Hall)] * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/2/9115.html Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror (Komaki City Hall)] * [https://apec.aichi-c.ed.jp/kyouka/shakai/kyouzai/2018/syakai/owari/owa042.htm Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun (Aichi ACE Net)]

'''Utsunomiya Shrine''' (宇都宮神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Komaki|en|Komaki|WD=Q389537|lt=Komaki, Aichi}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture}}. The shrine is notable for being the site of one of the largest {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in the Owari region, known as the '''Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun'''.

== History ==
* Late {{ill|3rd century|en|3rd century|WD=Q8101|lt=3rd century}} to early {{ill|4th century|en|4th century|WD=Q8099|lt=4th century}} - built of the {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}.
* Around {{ill|1929|en|1929|WD=Q18792|lt=1929}} (Showa 4) - Discovery of a {{ill|Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}.
* [[September 9, 1987]] (Showa 62) - The kofun was Historic Site1 a [[Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture]], and the mirror was Historic Site1 a [[designation1 of Aichi Prefecture]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/kouko/kensitei/0733.html|title=Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror|publisher=Aichi Prefecture|accessdate=2013-05-22}}</ref>

== Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun ==
{{Infobox ancient site
| name = Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun
| location = 
| coordinates = {{coord|35|16|46.4|N|136|53|46.8|E|region:JP|display=inline}}
| type = Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound
| map_size = Total length: 59m; height: approximately 6.5m; rear section: approximately 35m on each side
| Burial Facility = 
| burial goods = 
| associated_mounds = 
| built = Late 3rd century to early 4th century
| Interred_individual = 
| imperial_tombs = 
| historic_site = Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture
| tangible_notes = Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror (Historic Site1 by Aichi Prefecture)
}}

Located within the grounds of Utsunomiya Shrine, this is one of the largest {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in the Owari region. Part of the {{ill|Koki Kofun Cluster|ja|小木古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koki Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, it was constructed in the late {{ill|3rd century|en|3rd century|WD=Q8101|lt=3rd century}} to early {{ill|4th century|en|4th century|WD=Q8099|lt=4th century}}. The presence of the kofun indicates the prominence of a powerful local clan during that era. In addition to this kofun, several others exist in the vicinity. The mound is 59m in total length, 6.5m in height, and approximately 35m on each side of the rear section. A vertical stone chamber is located within. The site was Historic Site1 a Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture in {{ill|1987|en|1987|WD=Q2429|lt=1987}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/kinenbutu/siseki/kensitei/0923.html|title=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|publisher=Aichi Prefecture|accessdate=2013-05-22}}</ref>

<gallery>
File:Utsunomiya Jinjya Kofun 03.JPG|Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun
</gallery>

== Historic Site ==
* Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror (三角縁獣文帯三神三獣鏡) - A {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror}} excavated from the Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun. It was discovered around {{ill|1929|en|1929|WD=Q18792|lt=1929}} during the construction of the shrine hall at the front of the kofun and Historic Site1 a designation1 of Aichi Prefecture in {{ill|1987|en|1987|WD=Q2429|lt=1987}}.

== location ==
{{ill|Komaki|en|Komaki|WD=Q389537|lt=Komaki, Aichi}}, Ogi 3-chome 226.

== Access ==
* 5-minute walk from the {{ill|Ogi bus stop|ja|小木バス停留所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogi bus stop|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} served by {{ill|Meitetsu Bus|en|Meitetsu Bus|WD=Q6810397|lt=Meitetsu Bus}}.

== Nearby landmarks ==
* {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Road 25|ja|愛知県道25号春日井一宮線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefectural Road 25|1=Aichi Prefectural Road Route 25|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11494509}}
* {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Road 158|ja|愛知県道158号小口名古屋線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefectural Road 158|1=Aichi Prefectural Road Route 158|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11494413}}
* {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Road 451|ja|愛知県道451号名古屋外環状線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefectural Road 451|1=Aichi Prefectural Road Route 451|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11494718}}
* Jōon-ji Temple
* {{ill|Kōyashiki Kofun|ja|甲屋敷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōyashiki Kofun|qq=}}
* Seson-ji Temple
* Shintsū-ji Temple
* Ogi Elementary School (Komaki City)

It was discovered around {{ill|1929|en|1929|WD=Q18792|lt=1929}} during the construction of the shrine hall at the front of the kofun and Historic Site1 a designation1 of Aichi Prefecture in {{ill|1987|en|1987|WD=Q2429|lt=1987}}.

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] infobox | image = [[File:Utsunomiya Shrine.JPG|300px|Taken in [[:en:October 2009|October 2009]].]] | name = Utsunomiya Shrine | location = {{ill|Komaki|en|Komaki|WD=Q389537|lt=Komaki, Aichi|1=Komaki}}, Ogi 3-chome 226 | coordinates = {{coord|35|16|46.09|N|136|53|46.26|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} | Rank = {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.jinjahoncho.or.jp/5990|title=Utsunomiya Shrine|publisher=Association of Shinto Shrines|date=2020-03-12|accessdate=2022-04-04}}</ref> | establishment = | main_hall = | annual_festival = | rituals = }}
[[File:Utsunomiya Shrine 08.JPG|thumb|right|250px|Main shrine building.]]
'''Utsunomiya Shrine''' (宇都宮神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Komaki|en|Komaki|WD=Q389537|lt=Komaki, Aichi|1=Komaki}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. The shrine is notable for being the site of one of the largest {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in the Owari region, known as the '''Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun'''.
== History == * Late {{ill|3rd century|en|3rd century|WD=Q8101|lt=3rd century|1=3rd century}} to early {{ill|4th century|en|4th century|WD=Q8099|lt=4th century|1=4th century}} - built of the {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. * Around {{ill|1929|en|1929|WD=Q18792|lt=1929|1=1929}} (Showa 4) - Discovery of a {{ill|Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}. * [[September 9, 1987]] (Showa 62) - The kofun was Historic Site1 a [[Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture]], and the mirror was Historic Site1 a [[designation1 of Aichi Prefecture]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/kouko/kensitei/0733.html|title=Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror|publisher=Aichi Prefecture|accessdate=2013-05-22}}</ref>
== Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site | name = Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun | location = | coordinates = {{coord|35|16|46.4|N|136|53|46.8|E|region:JP|display=inline}} | type = Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound | map_size = Total length: 59m; height: approximately 6.5m; rear section: approximately 35m on each side | Burial Facility = | burial goods = | associated_mounds = | built = Late 3rd century to early 4th century | Interred_individual = | imperial_tombs = | historic_site = Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture | tangible_notes = Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror (Historic Site1 by Aichi Prefecture) }}
Located within the grounds of Utsunomiya Shrine, this is one of the largest {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in the Owari region. Part of the {{ill|Koki Kofun Cluster|ja|小木古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koki Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, it was constructed in the late {{ill|3rd century|en|3rd century|WD=Q8101|lt=3rd century|1=3rd century}} to early {{ill|4th century|en|4th century|WD=Q8099|lt=4th century|1=4th century}}. The presence of the kofun indicates the prominence of a powerful local clan during that era. In addition to this kofun, several others exist in the vicinity. The mound is 59m in total length, 6.5m in height, and approximately 35m on each side of the rear section. A vertical stone chamber is located within. The site was Historic Site1 a Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture in {{ill|1987|en|1987|WD=Q2429|lt=1987|1=1987}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/kinenbutu/siseki/kensitei/0923.html|title=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|publisher=Aichi Prefecture|accessdate=2013-05-22}}</ref>
<gallery> File:Utsunomiya Jinjya Kofun 03.JPG|Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun </gallery>
== Historic Site == * Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror (三角縁獣文帯三神三獣鏡) - A {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}} excavated from the Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun. It was discovered around {{ill|1929|en|1929|WD=Q18792|lt=1929|1=1929}} during the construction of the shrine hall at the front of the kofun and Historic Site1 a designation1 of Aichi Prefecture in {{ill|1987|en|1987|WD=Q2429|lt=1987|1=1987}}.
== location == {{ill|Komaki|en|Komaki|WD=Q389537|lt=Komaki, Aichi|1=Komaki}}, Ogi 3-chome 226.
== Access == * 5-minute walk from the {{ill|Ogi bus stop|ja|小木バス停留所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogi bus stop|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} served by {{ill|Meitetsu Bus|en|Meitetsu Bus|WD=Q6810397|lt=Meitetsu Bus|1=Meitetsu Bus}}.
== Nearby landmarks == * {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Road 25|ja|愛知県道25号春日井一宮線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefectural Road 25|1=Aichi Prefectural Road Route 25|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11494509}} * {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Road 158|ja|愛知県道158号小口名古屋線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefectural Road 158|1=Aichi Prefectural Road Route 158|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11494413}} * {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Road 451|ja|愛知県道451号名古屋外環状線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefectural Road 451|1=Aichi Prefectural Road Route 451|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11494718}} * Jōon-ji Temple * {{ill|Kōyashiki Kofun|ja|甲屋敷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōyashiki Kofun|qq=}} * Seson-ji Temple * Shintsū-ji Temple * Ogi Elementary School (Komaki City)

Located within the grounds of Utsunomiya Shrine, this is one of the largest {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in the Owari region. Part of the {{ill|Koki Kofun Cluster|ja|小木古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koki Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, it was constructed in the late {{ill|3rd century|en|3rd century|WD=Q8101|lt=3rd century|1=3rd century}} to early {{ill|4th century|en|4th century|WD=Q8099|lt=4th century|1=4th century}}. The presence of the kofun indicates the prominence of a powerful local clan during that era. In addition to this kofun, several others exist in the vicinity. The mound is 59m in total length, 6.5m in height, and approximately 35m on each side of the rear section. A vertical stone chamber is located within. The site was Historic Site1 a Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture in {{ill|1987|en|1987|WD=Q2429|lt=1987|1=1987}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/kinenbutu/siseki/kensitei/0923.html|title=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|publisher=Aichi Prefecture|accessdate=2013-05-22}}</ref>

It was discovered around {{ill|1929|en|1929|WD=Q18792|lt=1929|1=1929}} during the construction of the shrine hall at the front of the kofun and Historic Site1 a designation1 of Aichi Prefecture in {{ill|1987|en|1987|WD=Q2429|lt=1987|1=1987}}.

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] infobox | image = [[File:Utsunomiya Shrine.JPG|300px|Taken in [[:en:October 2009|October 2009]].]] | name = Utsunomiya Shrine | location = {{ill|Komaki|en|Komaki|WD=Q389537|lt=Komaki, Aichi|1=Komaki}}, Ogi 3-chome 226 | coordinates = {{coord|35|16|46.09|N|136|53|46.26|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} | Rank = {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.jinjahoncho.or.jp/5990|title=Utsunomiya Shrine|publisher=Association of Shinto Shrines|date=2020-03-12|accessdate=2022-04-04}}</ref> | establishment = | main_hall = | annual_festival = | rituals = }}
[[File:Utsunomiya Shrine 08.JPG|thumb|right|250px|Main shrine building.]]
'''Utsunomiya Shrine''' (宇都宮神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Komaki|en|Komaki|WD=Q389537|lt=Komaki, Aichi|1=Komaki}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. The shrine is notable for being the site of one of the largest {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in the Owari region, known as the '''Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun'''.
== History == * Late {{ill|3rd century|en|3rd century|WD=Q8101|lt=3rd century|1=3rd century}} to early {{ill|4th century|en|4th century|WD=Q8099|lt=4th century|1=4th century}} - built of the {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. * Around {{ill|1929|en|1929|WD=Q18792|lt=1929|1=1929}} (Showa 4) - Discovery of a {{ill|Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}. * [[September 9, 1987]] (Showa 62) - The kofun was Historic Site1 a [[Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture]], and the mirror was Historic Site1 a [[designation1 of Aichi Prefecture]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/kouko/kensitei/0733.html|title=Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror|publisher=Aichi Prefecture|accessdate=2013-05-22}}</ref>
== Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site | name = Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun | location = | coordinates = {{coord|35|16|46.4|N|136|53|46.8|E|region:JP|display=inline}} | type = Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound | map_size = Total length: 59m; height: approximately 6.5m; rear section: approximately 35m on each side | Burial Facility = | burial goods = | associated_mounds = | built = Late 3rd century to early 4th century | Interred_individual = | imperial_tombs = | historic_site = Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture | tangible_notes = Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror (Historic Site1 by Aichi Prefecture) }}
Located within the grounds of Utsunomiya Shrine, this is one of the largest {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in the Owari region. Part of the {{ill|Koki Kofun Cluster|ja|小木古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koki Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, it was constructed in the late {{ill|3rd century|en|3rd century|WD=Q8101|lt=3rd century|1=3rd century}} to early {{ill|4th century|en|4th century|WD=Q8099|lt=4th century|1=4th century}}. The presence of the kofun indicates the prominence of a powerful local clan during that era. In addition to this kofun, several others exist in the vicinity. The mound is 59m in total length, 6.5m in height, and approximately 35m on each side of the rear section. A vertical stone chamber is located within. The site was Historic Site1 a Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture in {{ill|1987|en|1987|WD=Q2429|lt=1987|1=1987}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/kinenbutu/siseki/kensitei/0923.html|title=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|publisher=Aichi Prefecture|accessdate=2013-05-22}}</ref>
<gallery> File:Utsunomiya Jinjya Kofun 03.JPG|Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun </gallery>
== Historic Site == * Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror (三角縁獣文帯三神三獣鏡) - A {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}} excavated from the Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun. It was discovered around {{ill|1929|en|1929|WD=Q18792|lt=1929|1=1929}} during the construction of the shrine hall at the front of the kofun and Historic Site1 a designation1 of Aichi Prefecture in {{ill|1987|en|1987|WD=Q2429|lt=1987|1=1987}}.
== location == {{ill|Komaki|en|Komaki|WD=Q389537|lt=Komaki, Aichi|1=Komaki}}, Ogi 3-chome 226.
== Access == * 5-minute walk from the {{ill|Ogi bus stop|ja|小木バス停留所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogi bus stop|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} served by {{ill|Meitetsu Bus|en|Meitetsu Bus|WD=Q6810397|lt=Meitetsu Bus|1=Meitetsu Bus}}.
== Nearby landmarks == * {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Road 25|ja|愛知県道25号春日井一宮線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefectural Road 25|1=Aichi Prefectural Road Route 25|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11494509}} * {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Road 158|ja|愛知県道158号小口名古屋線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefectural Road 158|1=Aichi Prefectural Road Route 158|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11494413}} * {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Road 451|ja|愛知県道451号名古屋外環状線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefectural Road 451|1=Aichi Prefectural Road Route 451|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11494718}} * Jōon-ji Temple * {{ill|Kōyashiki Kofun|ja|甲屋敷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōyashiki Kofun|qq=}} * Seson-ji Temple * Shintsū-ji Temple * Ogi Elementary School (Komaki City)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Koki Kofun Cluster|ja|小木古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koki Kofun Cluster|qq=}} - A general term for the group of kofun in this area. * {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine|ja|宇都宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shrine|qq=}}
== External links == {{Commonscat|Utsunomiya Shrine}} {{Commonscat|Utsunomiya Jinjya Kofun|宇都宮神社古墳}} * [https://www.utunomiyajinjya-komaki.com/ Utsunomiya Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/2/9111.html Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun (Komaki City Hall)] * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazai/1_1/2/bunkazai/shiteibunkazai/2/9115.html Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror (Komaki City Hall)] * [https://apec.aichi-c.ed.jp/kyouka/shakai/kyouzai/2018/syakai/owari/owa042.htm Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun (Aichi ACE Net)]

'''Utsunomiya Shrine''' (宇都宮神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Komaki|en|Komaki|WD=Q389537|lt=Komaki, Aichi|1=Komaki}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. The shrine is notable for being the site of one of the largest {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in the Owari region, known as the '''Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun'''.
== History == * Late {{ill|3rd century|en|3rd century|WD=Q8101|lt=3rd century|1=3rd century}} to early {{ill|4th century|en|4th century|WD=Q8099|lt=4th century|1=4th century}} - built of the {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rectangular keyhole-shaped mound|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. * Around {{ill|1929|en|1929|WD=Q18792|lt=1929|1=1929}} (Showa 4) - Discovery of a {{ill|Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}. * [[September 9, 1987]] (Showa 62) - The kofun was Historic Site1 a [[Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture]], and the mirror was Historic Site1 a [[designation1 of Aichi Prefecture]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/kouko/kensitei/0733.html|title=Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror|publisher=Aichi Prefecture|accessdate=2013-05-22}}</ref>
== Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site | name = Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun | location = | coordinates = {{coord|35|16|46.4|N|136|53|46.8|E|region:JP|display=inline}} | type = Rectangular keyhole-shaped mound | map_size = Total length: 59m; height: approximately 6.5m; rear section: approximately 35m on each side | Burial Facility = | burial goods = | associated_mounds = | built = Late 3rd century to early 4th century | Interred_individual = | imperial_tombs = | historic_site = Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture | tangible_notes = Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror (Historic Site1 by Aichi Prefecture) }}
Located within the grounds of Utsunomiya Shrine, this is one of the largest {{ill|rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rectangular keyhole-shaped mounds|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in the Owari region. Part of the {{ill|Koki Kofun Cluster|ja|小木古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koki Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, it was constructed in the late {{ill|3rd century|en|3rd century|WD=Q8101|lt=3rd century|1=3rd century}} to early {{ill|4th century|en|4th century|WD=Q8099|lt=4th century|1=4th century}}. The presence of the kofun indicates the prominence of a powerful local clan during that era. In addition to this kofun, several others exist in the vicinity. The mound is 59m in total length, 6.5m in height, and approximately 35m on each side of the rear section. A vertical stone chamber is located within. The site was Historic Site1 a Historic Site of Aichi Prefecture in {{ill|1987|en|1987|WD=Q2429|lt=1987|1=1987}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/kinenbutu/siseki/kensitei/0923.html|title=Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun|publisher=Aichi Prefecture|accessdate=2013-05-22}}</ref>
<gallery> File:Utsunomiya Jinjya Kofun 03.JPG|Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun </gallery>
== Historic Site == * Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror (三角縁獣文帯三神三獣鏡) - A {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}} excavated from the Utsunomiya Shrine Kofun. It was discovered around {{ill|1929|en|1929|WD=Q18792|lt=1929|1=1929}} during the construction of the shrine hall at the front of the kofun and Historic Site1 a designation1 of Aichi Prefecture in {{ill|1987|en|1987|WD=Q2429|lt=1987|1=1987}}.
== location == {{ill|Komaki|en|Komaki|WD=Q389537|lt=Komaki, Aichi|1=Komaki}}, Ogi 3-chome 226.
== Access == * 5-minute walk from the {{ill|Ogi bus stop|ja|小木バス停留所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogi bus stop|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} served by {{ill|Meitetsu Bus|en|Meitetsu Bus|WD=Q6810397|lt=Meitetsu Bus|1=Meitetsu Bus}}.
== Nearby landmarks == * {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Road 25|ja|愛知県道25号春日井一宮線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefectural Road 25|1=Aichi Prefectural Road Route 25|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11494509}} * {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Road 158|ja|愛知県道158号小口名古屋線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefectural Road 158|1=Aichi Prefectural Road Route 158|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11494413}} * {{ill|Aichi Prefectural Road 451|ja|愛知県道451号名古屋外環状線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aichi Prefectural Road 451|1=Aichi Prefectural Road Route 451|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11494718}} * Jōon-ji Temple * {{ill|Kōyashiki Kofun|ja|甲屋敷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōyashiki Kofun|qq=}} * Seson-ji Temple * Shintsū-ji Temple * Ogi Elementary School (Komaki City)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Koki Kofun Cluster|ja|小木古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koki Kofun Cluster|qq=}} - A general term for the group of kofun in this area. * {{ill|Utsunomiya Shrine|ja|宇都宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Shrine|qq=}}
